data.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification, overlay): PASS. No findings.

The bright-line fires (novel testing architecture + external-integration capability assertions), so the overlay applies. Evaluated against all three T1 sub-checks the plan PASSES; recording affirmations for later passes.

AFFIRMATIONS (T1 sub-checks satisfied):

(a) Relevant prior art is considered before committing — STRONGLY satisfied. The Context section runs an explicit, named prior-art survey before choosing the approach: Pact (consumer-driven contracts), vcrpy (semantic matchers / layered redaction), Schemathesis + Dredd/Gavel (structure-not-value validation), the Google SWE-book "verified fake" + its change-detector warning, and a concrete comparison of how real Jira clients test (jira-python's live Dockerized/Cloud sandbox vs jira-cli's on-disk fixtures captured from real Jira served through production serialization via an in-process mock). The chosen design is plainly derived from jira-cli's pattern + the SWE-book verified-fake prescription rather than reinvented. Not reinventing/repackaging.

(b) Novelty justified vs an established approach (anti-repackaging / Rule-of-Three) — satisfied. The plan does NOT claim a novel architecture; it deliberately right-sizes a known pattern and explicitly REJECTS the heavier established options with a stated rationale: "Full live-sandbox e2e and cross-team Pact are out of scope (overkill for a single team)," and "explicitly avoiding change-detector bloat." Story C (59ef) grounds the verified-fake choice directly in "Software Engineering at Google." The single-team scoping is the justification for not adopting Pact/live-sandbox — a sound established-vs-tailored tradeoff, not unjustified novelty.

(c) Unverified capability assertions resolved — satisfied. The library capabilities relied on are either already in-repo (the Jira REST enrichment surface — search_issues, get_comment_map, get_issuelinks_map, get_parent_map — and the existing external/live pytest marker, both confirmed in production use) or correctly gated. The ONE externally-new capability assertion — "validate the real client's responses against Jira Cloud's published OpenAPI where the spec covers the field" (story 59ef "What") — is NOT left load-bearing: it is explicitly marked **Optional**, caveated "investigate spec completeness first," and deferred to a named **epic open question** (i.e. a spike). Per T1(c)/decisiveness, an unverified capability routed to a spike before commitment is the correct handling, so no finding is warranted; the only nudge (confirm-or-drop the OpenAPI leg after the spike) is already captured by the T3 reviewer on 59ef and need not be duplicated here.

Net: T1 is one of the better-handled criteria in this plan — a real prior-art survey, an explicit established-vs-tailored justification, and the lone novel external capability kept behind a spike. Clean PASS.
